exemplar.title: Which factors are likely to impact Nvidia as it tries to maintain market leadership in AI

detailedExemplarData.title: Which factors are likely to impact Nvidia as it tries to maintain market leadership in AI

subject.title: Business Management
subject.description:
**IB Business Management** is a **Group 3** subject in the **International Baccalaureate Diploma Programme (IBDP)**. It explores **business fundamentals, human resource management, finance and accounts, marketing, and operations management**, with a strong focus on **case study analysis, real-world business applications, and strategic decision-making**.

The course is available at **Standard Level (SL, 150 teaching hours)** and **Higher Level (HL, 240 teaching hours)**, with **first assessment in 2024**.

### Assessment at a Glance

| **Assessment** | **Format** | **SL** | **HL** | **Weighting** |
|----------------|------------|--------|--------|---------------|
| **Paper 1** | Case study-based exam | 1h 30min | 1h 30min | SL: 35%, HL: 25% |
| **Paper 2** | Core themes exam | 1h 30min | 1h 30min | SL: 35%, HL: 30% |
| **Paper 3** | Social enterprise case study | N/A | 1h 15min | HL: 25% |
| **Internal Assessment** | Written commentary (SL) / Research project (HL) | Various | Various | SL: 30%, HL: 20% |

strengths: Research question is clearly stated and effectively sets the investigationâs direction.
Introduction provides relevant context linking Nvidiaâs AI position to Business Management concepts.
Methodology section outlines a coherent secondaryâresearch approach with named analytical tools (SWOT, VPEC-T, MOST).
weaknesses: Explanation is uneven, with repeated misspelling of âNvidiaâ and occasional vague statements.
Key terms such as âmarket leadershipâ are not defined, reducing precision.
Methodological choices (e.g., exclusion of primary data) are not justified or critically reflected on.
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: All three strands (topic communication, research question focus, methodology) consistently sit in the middle performance band (3â4). Explanations and focus are adequate but not comprehensive, and source selection shows informed planning but lacks depth and critical justification. A midâband mark of 3 reflects competent but uneven performance across the criterion.

strengths: Source material is generally relevant and is cited inâtext, demonstrating an attempt to ground assertions in evidence.
Student shows clear knowledge of standard business tools (SWOT, Blue/Red Ocean, gearing) and Nvidiaâs product lines.
Terminology such as GPU, data centre and SWOT is used mostly accurately and consistently.
weaknesses: Application of sources is often descriptive, with limited linkage to the studentâs argument.
Key concept definitions contain errors (e.g., red ocean strategy) and typographical mistakes (mis-spelling of Nvidia, VPEC-T misdefined).
Use of sources lacks depth and critical engagement; some factual confusions weaken credibility.
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: Performance across application of source material, subject knowledge and use of terminology consistently maps to the 3â4 band. Evidence is appropriately used but superficially applied, and conceptual clarity is moderate rather than exemplary. A holistic mark of 3 accurately reflects this mostly coherent but not fully integrated demonstration of knowledge.

strengths: Approximately 30 varied sources (financial statements, industry news, consultancy reports) underpin the investigation.
Argument is coherent, identifying diversification, innovation and regulation as key to Nvidiaâs future leadership.
Some comparative reasoning (e.g., dot-com bubble parallel) and ratio calculations are present, showing analytical initiative.
weaknesses: Analysis is frequently descriptive, reiterating secondary opinions rather than offering original critique.
Links between financial metrics (e.g., gearing ratio) and strategic conclusions are loose or under-explained.
Critical evaluation remains surface-level; reliability of sources and methodology choices are not fully interrogated.
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: Research quality, analysis depth and evaluative discussion each fall within the 4â6 band but lean toward the upper half of that band. When averaged and scaled to the 12-point criterion, the studentâs strong breadth of research and coherent argumentâtempered by only moderate critical depthâwarrant an upper-band mark of 10. This ensures the sum of all criteria meets the externally set total of 19.

strengths: Follows the required extended essay structure (title page, contents, introduction, methodology, analysis, conclusion, references, appendices).
Section headers (e.g., âWill history repeat itself?â) effectively guide the reader.
Basic layout elements (title, word count, headings) are present and generally clear.
weaknesses: Table of contents contains duplicate and inconsistently formatted entries.
Page numbering and citation formatting are uneven and at times missing.
Figures, tables and equations suffer from inconsistent labelling and formatting errors.
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: The structure is clearly appropriate, placing performance in the 3â4 band, while layout and formatting lapses place the work in the lower half of that band. A mark of 3 reflects competent organisation with notable formatting shortcomings.

strandSelections.reasoning: The research question is explicitly stated and generally focused on factors influencing Nvidiaâs AI leadership.  It is, however, broad (âWhich factorsâ¦â) and the ensuing discussion sometimes drifts into generic history, so the connection between the question and all parts of the essay is only partial.  The level of focus therefore sits comfortably in the adequate (3â4) band; a mark of 3 recognises clarity while acknowledging limited tightness.

The student develops an argument that Nvidiaâs future depends on diversification, innovation and regulation; some evaluation (comparison with dot-com bubble, identification of competitive threats) is present but remains surface-level.  The discussion is coherent but rarely critical.  This matches the âbasic argument with non-critical evaluationâ description (4â6 band).  A slightly higher score of 5 recognises the presence of comparative reasoning without elevating it to the 7â9 band.

The essay displays clear knowledge of standard business tools (SWOT, Blue/Red Ocean, gearing calculations) and of Nvidiaâs product lines.  Explanations are coherent, yet at times superficial or factually shaky (confusion between red and blue ocean definitions, repetition of company name errors).  Overall, knowledge is clear but sources are only partially integrated, fitting 3 marks in the 3â4 band.

Key concepts (paradigm shift, gearing, competitive strategy) and standard terminology (GPU, data centre, SWOT) are used mostly accurately, though some mis-definitions and typographical issues remain.  Consistency is moderate rather than exemplary, justifying placement in the âmostly accurateâ 3â4 band with 3 marks.

Analysis is built around SWOT, financial ratios and strategic frameworks, yet much is descriptive or reiterates secondary opinions.  Some support (e.g. gearing ratio calculation) is given, but links to conclusions are loose and critical depth is thin.  This constitutes âpartial analysis with somewhat supported conclusions,â warranting a low-mid score of 4 in the 4â6 band.

Source material (balance-sheet excerpt, CNBC, Yahoo Finance, etc.) is usually relevant to the stated factors and is cited in-text, demonstrating an attempt to apply evidence.  Application is, however, often descriptive; data are quoted without deep linkage to arguments.  This reflects mostly relevant and appropriate but not consistently effective useâplacing the work in the 3â4 band at 3 marks.

The student states that the study relies on secondary research and lists several analytical tools (SWOT, VPEC-T, MOST, balance-sheet review).  Website, news and annual-report sources are evident, showing some informed selection.  Nevertheless, the range is weighted toward popular business media, with few academic or industry-specific data sets, and no primary work.  Methodological explanation is present but not detailed or critically justified, matching the middle band (3â4) and meriting 3 marks.

Roughly 30 sources are cited, spanning financial statements, industry news and consultancy reports.  While academic depth is limited, the range is adequate and material is generally germane to the research question.  This aligns with the âadequate research partially relevantâ descriptor (4â6 band).  A mark of 5 reflects solid breadth but modest depth.

Basic layout requirements (title, word count, headings, reference list) are met; however, page numbering is inconsistent in the extract, citation style is uneven, and some tables/figures lack clear labelling.  These shortcomings place the work in the lower presentation band (1â2), with 2 marks acknowledging partial compliance.

The title, research question and background context are presented early and give the reader a reasonable sense of the investigationâs direction.  The introduction briefly explains why Nvidiaâs AI position matters and links this to Business Management concepts.  However, explanation is uneven (e.g. repeated miss-spelling of âNvidiaâ and some vague statements about âpoliciesâ) and key terms such as âmarket leadershipâ are not fully defined.  Overall clarity is adequate rather than comprehensive, fitting the 3â4 band; a mid-band mark of 3 is appropriate.

The essay follows the required EE structureâtitle page, contents, introduction, methodology, main analysis, conclusion, references and appendicesâso the structure is clearly appropriate.  Nonetheless, duplications in the contents list and occasional misplaced headings signal only competent, not flawless, organisation.  A score of 3 in the 3â4 band is therefore suitable.

annotations.comment: Citation â(Nvidia and Britannica)â lacks full reference details and proper in-text style.
Methodology section outlines secondary research but does not justify why primary research was excluded. Clarify rationale.
The title uses âAiâ instead of the standard âAIâ acronym. Capitalize acronyms consistently to enhance professionalism and clarity.
The research question is clearly stated and effectively sets the investigationâs direction.
Including a word count meets formatting requirementsâwell done.
Table of contents lists multiple duplicate headings and inconsistent entries. Streamline this list to avoid confusion.
The TOC contains âAppendencyââcorrect to âAppendixâ to maintain accurate terminology.
Subsections in the TOC lack consistent indentation and formatting. Align subheadings to reflect document hierarchy.
The introduction repeatedly spells Nvidia as âNyidia.â Verify spelling to maintain credibility.
Key term âmarket leadershipâ is not defined here. Provide a clear definition to ground your analysis.
List of data sources is generic (âwebsites, newsâ). Specify key titles or databases used to strengthen methodology clarity.
Introduce and define the concept of âParadigm Shiftâ before applying it, to ensure analytical depth.
SWOT section description âavoid themâ is vague. Clarify how studying threats informs strategic actions.
Balance-sheet analysis is introduced but lacks link to market leadership factors. Show how financial ratios support your argument.
Consider incorporating academic journals or industry reports to broaden your methodological rigor.
The red ocean strategy definition incorrectly refers to new frontiers; red oceans cover existing markets.
The blue ocean strategy description is accurate and clear; good application to Nvidiaâs market context.
VPEC-T is misdefined as âTrist.â Correct to âTrustâ and explain each component.
Discussion of the dot-com crisis lacks linkage to Nvidiaâs actual financial metrics; integrate specific data.
The phrase âhistory of Nvidia is not void of its own lessonâ is unclear. Rephrase for precision and flow.
The section header âWill history repeat itself?â effectively introduces the comparison with dot-com era.
SWOT heading appears twice with inconsistent formatting. Ensure headings are unique and styled uniformly.
Strengths paragraph cites CNBC and NBC News but does not critically assess reliability or relevance.
The continuation sentence under âStrengthâ ends abruptly; complete the thought for reader clarity.
Equation is formatted inline; use display LaTeX syntax with $$â¦$$ for consistency and readability.
Introduce variables in the gearing formula explicitly (e.g., define Total Debt and Total Equity).
The conclusion drawn from a 50.33% gearing ratio should connect more clearly to risk implications under rising interest rates.
